hyperedges.label: ICT Core Trade Sequence: BOS + FVG (within Displacement) + OB = Entry
Gap Pattern: ICT Structure + No Experiment + No Backtest = Disconnected Cluster Gap
Research Pipeline: Gap Engine Protocol → Question to Insight → Insight to Experiment
ICT Price Delivery Mechanism: Displacement creates BOS and FVG, then rebalances via OB/FVG
Kronos Tokenization Pipeline: OHLCVA → BSQ → Hierarchical Coarse+Fine Tokens
Multi-Timeframe Structural Bias: Market Structure + Fractal Alignment + Session Timing
BOS Scoring Validation Pipeline: Method → Experiment → Insight
Wavelet Jump Model Stack: HMM Regime + Wavelet Features + LightGBM
ICT Structural Concepts: Liquidity + BOS + FVG Form the Setup Framework

properties.description: Dark-themed candlestick chart showing price action with multiple annotated horizontal levels and structural elements
properties.color_scheme: dark background, green bullish candles, red bearish candles

properties.description: Red horizontal line marking the swing high resistance area in the upper portion of the chart, acting as a premium zone or liquidity level
properties.position: upper chart area, spanning from left swing high through central rally peak

properties.description: Red horizontal line in the middle-upper section of the chart, marking a second key resistance or prior swing high level
properties.position: mid-upper area, near central rally high

properties.description: Red horizontal line at the bottom of the chart spanning the full width, marking the major support or discount zone / buy-side liquidity level
properties.position: lower chart area, near absolute swing low

nodes.label: Fair Value Gap or Order Block (Upper White Box)

properties.description: White horizontal rectangle/box in the upper-central region of the chart, marking a Fair Value Gap (FVG) or Order Block (OB) that price reacted to
properties.position: upper-central area, overlapping with the rally structure

nodes.norm_label: fair value gap or order block (lower white box)

nodes.label: Fair Value Gap or Order Block (Left White Box)

properties.description: White horizontal rectangle/box on the left portion of the chart, marking a FVG or OB zone corresponding to the initial downmove

properties.description: Dashed rectangle on the right side of the chart indicating a liquidity pool, demand zone, or unmitigated order block that price approached from above
properties.position: right side, below the post-distribution swing low

nodes.norm_label: dashed rectangle zone (liquidity pool or ob)

properties.description: Initial swing high visible on the left side of the chart before the major downswing; marks a liquidity pool (sell-side liquidity above highs)
The absolute swing low of the chart, coinciding with the lower red support line; represents a discount zone and potential buy-side liquidity pool
Clear downward price movement from the initial swing high to the major swing low, indicating bearish market structure (lower highs, lower lows)
Strong bullish recovery from the major swing low back up to test resistance levels, suggesting a BOS (Break of Structure) to the upside or a relief rally
Bearish price action following the rally peak, indicating distribution or a rejection at resistance, leading to a secondary selloff
Implied BOS event where price breaks a prior swing point, changing the market structure bias; inferred from the rally exceeding intermediate highs
Price action that sweeps above a prior swing high or below a prior swing low to capture resting liquidity (stop orders) before reversing; visible at both the swing low and rally peak
ICT concept: a three-candle imbalance where the first candle's high/low does not overlap with the third candle's low/high, leaving an unmitigated gap in price; indicated by white boxes
ICT concept: the last opposing candle before a significant move; acts as a supply or demand zone where institutional orders are placed; marked by white boxes and the dashed rectangle
ICT framework dividing price ranges into premium (above 50% equilibrium, sell) and discount (below 50% equilibrium, buy) zones; the red lines delineate the range boundaries
A change in the prevailing market structure, from bearish (downswing) to bullish (rally), inferred from the price recovering from the major swing low
Areas of sideways price action visible mid-chart during the recovery phase, representing accumulation or re-distribution before the next directional move
A candlestick chart displaying red and green candles showing price action over time, with a predominantly bearish/downtrending structure
Overall price action shows a bearish downtrend with a sequence of lower highs and lower lows across the chart
Multiple white horizontal lines drawn at significant price points — likely swing highs, swing lows, resistance, or liquidity pool markers
Price reversal points at local peaks, marked by white horizontal lines; in a downtrend these become resistance levels
properties.ict_context: In ICT methodology, swing highs are liquidity pools targeted by smart money

properties.description: Price reversal points at local troughs, marked by white horizontal lines; in a downtrend these are taken out sequentially as the market makes lower lows
properties.ict_context: In ICT methodology, swing lows hold sell-side liquidity that smart money targets

properties.description: White horizontal lines likely represent stop-loss clusters above swing highs and below swing lows — key ICT concept of buy-side and sell-side liquidity
A dashed horizontal line visible near the mid-section of the chart, potentially marking a 50% retracement equilibrium, a prior level of interest, or a mean reference
properties.ict_context: Could represent equilibrium (50% of a range), a previous daily/weekly level, or an OTE (Optimal Trade Entry) reference

properties.description: Vertical semi-transparent gray shaded bands separating distinct time periods — likely marking trading sessions (London, NY, Asia) or daily boundaries
properties.ict_context: ICT places heavy emphasis on session timing: London Open, NY Open, NY Midnight Open, and killzones

properties.description: High-probability ICT trading windows aligned with major session opens; the vertical bands in the chart may delineate these killzones
Sequential pattern of lower highs followed by lower lows confirming a bearish trend; visible across the chart structure
properties.significance: Structural confirmation of bearish trend; in ICT, BOS (Break of Structure) to the downside at each swing low validates the trend

properties.description: Each time price takes out a prior swing low in this downtrend, a BOS to the downside is implied, confirming bearish market structure shift
Prior swing highs that now act as resistance; in the downtrend, price rallies into these levels and reverses lower
properties.ict_context: Premium price areas where institutions distribute / sell; optimal for short entries in bearish bias

properties.description: The study of raw price movement through candlestick patterns without lagging indicators; the chart uses pure price action with structural annotations
The overall analytical framework implied by the chart annotations: horizontal levels marking liquidity, session bands, and structural breaks — hallmarks of ICT/SMC methodology
properties.author_association: Inner Circle Trader (ICT) / Michael J. Huddleston

properties.description: Stop-loss orders clustered below swing lows; price is drawn toward these levels in a downtrend to trigger retail long stop-losses before further decline
Blue dotted vertical lines forming a time grid across the chart, aiding in time-based analysis and session alignment
properties.visual: blue dotted vertical lines at regular intervals

properties.description: Full candlestick chart showing two-phase market structure with bearish and bullish zones, dotted horizontal levels, and W-pattern price action
Dark red/maroon shaded background region on the left half of the chart indicating a bearish manipulation or distribution phase
Dark green shaded background region on the right half of the chart indicating a bullish accumulation or markup phase
Multiple white dotted horizontal lines marking key price levels throughout the chart — likely equal highs, equal lows, swing points, or liquidity pools
properties.count: approximately 10+ dotted lines at various price levels

properties.description: Solid white horizontal line near the bottom of the chart acting as a major support or base level; price wicked below it before strong bullish reversal
A double-bottom or W-shaped price pattern within the bearish/red zone, showing two swing lows with a bounce between them — classic liquidity sweep and reversal structure
A second W-shaped double bottom pattern in the bullish/green zone, followed by a strong bullish breakout to the upside
Price wicks below key dotted levels and the solid white support line, sweeping sell-side liquidity (stop losses) before reversing sharply bullish — a classic ICT smart money concept
Several dotted rectangular regions at key price levels marking potential order block zones — areas where institutional orders were placed and price is expected to react on return
Bullish Break of Structure visible as price in the right zone breaks above previous swing highs and trends higher, confirming bullish market structure
Dotted lines at the top of the chart marking equal highs — buy-side liquidity resting above these levels where stop orders accumulate, targeted by smart money
Dotted lines at swing low regions marking equal lows — sell-side liquidity resting below these levels, swept during the downside wicks before reversals
The chart is divided into two distinct phases: a bearish/distribution phase (left, red shading) transitioning into a bullish/accumulation and markup phase (right, green shading)
The full chart narrative illustrates an ICT smart money reversal sequence: price sweeps sell-side liquidity at the bottom, then reverses strongly to the upside in the bullish zone

nodes.label: Candlestick Price Chart
nodes.notes: Dark-background candlestick chart showing red (bearish) and teal/green (bullish) candles across a visible price range.

nodes.label: V-Shape Price Reversal
nodes.notes: The chart exhibits a clear V-shaped structure: sustained downtrend followed by a sharp reversal and uptrend to new highs.

nodes.label: Downtrend Leg (Bearish Swing)
nodes.notes: Left side of chart shows a series of lower highs and lower lows, predominantly red candles indicating selling pressure.

nodes.label: Uptrend Leg (Bullish Reversal Swing)
nodes.notes: Right side of chart shows a strong bullish recovery with higher highs and higher lows, predominantly teal/green candles. Reaches back near or above original starting price level.

nodes.label: Swing Low / Price Trough
nodes.notes: The lowest point of the V-shape reversal. A prominent bearish candle with a long lower wick reaches an extreme low before reversal. This is the key turning point.

nodes.label: Horizontal Support / Liquidity Level
nodes.notes: A white horizontal line is drawn near the swing low area, spanning several candles. This marks a key price level — likely a support zone, previous consolidation low, or liquidity pool that was swept before reversal.

nodes.label: Liquidity Sweep / Stop Hunt
nodes.notes: The price action dips below the horizontal support line (the white line) with a long wick, suggesting a liquidity grab below sell-side liquidity before the bullish reversal. Classic ICT stop hunt pattern.

nodes.label: Sell-Side Liquidity (SSL)
nodes.notes: Sell-side liquidity resting below the horizontal support line. The market sweeps this level to trigger stop losses before reversing bullish.

nodes.label: Break of Structure (BOS) — Bullish
nodes.notes: After the liquidity sweep at the swing low, price breaks above the prior swing high on the right side, confirming a bullish break of structure (BOS) and shift in market structure.

nodes.label: Market Structure Shift (MSS)
nodes.notes: The transition from bearish market structure (downtrend) to bullish market structure (uptrend) occurring at the swing low after the liquidity sweep.

nodes.label: Bearish Candles (Red)
nodes.notes: Red candles dominating the downtrend leg of the V-shape.

nodes.label: Bullish Candles (Teal/Green)
nodes.notes: Teal/green candles dominating the recovery/uptrend leg of the V-shape, showing strong bullish momentum.

nodes.label: Lower Wick Rejection at Swing Low
nodes.notes: The swing low candle has a prominent lower wick indicating sharp rejection of lower prices — consistent with a liquidity sweep and institutional buying.
nodes.norm_label: lower wick rejection at swing low

nodes.label: Pre-Drop Consolidation / Distribution
nodes.notes: Left portion of the chart shows choppy, overlapping candles before the sustained drop begins — possible distribution or consolidation before the bearish move.

properties.description: Break of Structure on the 4-hour chart, annotated with dashed horizontal line marking the broken structure level
Overall market structure visible in chart: downtrend followed by bullish BOS, rally to swing high, then retracement and partial recovery
White horizontal line on the left side of the chart marking a prior swing low or support level
White horizontal line at the bottom of the chart representing the major structural low or key support
White horizontal line in the middle-right area marking a support or demand zone after the second sell-off
Upper blue horizontal line on the right side of chart marking a key resistance or supply zone
Lower blue horizontal line just below the upper blue line, forming a resistance zone cluster in the right portion of the chart
Price action showing a strong bullish move after the BOS 4H level was broken, indicating a change of character or shift in market direction
Initial bearish price movement from a swing high on the left side down to the structural low at the bottom of the chart

links.description: The BTC chart on Binance is the instrument on which the ICT methodology is being applied
Fibonacci retracement tool is drawn directly on the BTC chart to mark key levels
Liquidity sweep below prior lows precedes bullish reaction from the order block — classic ICT setup where liquidity is taken before OB mitigation
Price reacts from the bullish OB and enters a consolidation/ranging phase above the OB zone
The BOS from the sharp drop creates the OB zone; the bullish BOS after OB mitigation confirms temporary structural shift
FVG and OB often co-exist in ICT — the rapid move that creates the OB typically also leaves a FVG that may be partially filled
ICT framework defines and uses order blocks as key supply/demand zones
The descending trendline represents the prior bearish structure that terminates with the liquidity sweep at the lows
Liquidity sweeps (stop hunts) are a core ICT concept — smart money takes out retail stops before reversing
The 50% Fibonacci level is explicitly labeled as a key reference at 26,114.33
The consolidation zone is anchored around the 50% equilibrium level, which acts as a magnet for price
ICT uses equilibrium (50% of a range) to identify optimal entry zones — price often rebalances to equilibrium before continuing
After failing to sustain above equilibrium during consolidation, price breaks down bearishly
BOS is a core ICT/SMC concept used to identify shifts in market structure
FVG is a fundamental ICT concept representing price inefficiency / imbalance zones
SMC is derived from / closely aligned with ICT methodology; both analyze institutional order flow through OBs, FVGs, and liquidity
